# Dewatermark Platform

> Open source, local-first AI dewatermark platform for asynchronous video watermark removal.

This repository provides a self-hosted API and worker system for video dewatermark jobs. It is designed for local deployment, provider routing, callback handling, and ComfyUI-based AI runtime integration.

## Primary Docs

- [README](./README.md): Main project homepage in Chinese with quick start and positioning
- [README.en](./README.en.md): English project summary
- [Docs Index](./docs/index.md): Documentation entrypoint
- [Overview](./docs/overview.md): What the project is, who it is for, and current scope
- [FAQ](./docs/faq.md): Direct Q&A for common retrieval queries
- [Architecture](./docs/architecture.md): Service boundaries and data model
- [API](./docs/api.md): HTTP API contract draft
- [Requirements](./docs/requirements.md): Product requirements and non-goals
- [Roadmap](./docs/roadmap.md): Delivery phases and current status

## Key Terms

- open source AI watermark remover
- AI video dewatermark platform
- self-hosted watermark removal API
- local-first video watermark removal
- ComfyUI dewatermark workflow
- async job-based media processing

## Current Truths

- The project is video-first.
- The intended AI-first provider is `comfy_diffueraser`.
- `local_fallback` exists to keep the platform runnable when the AI runtime is not ready.
- The repository is focused on local deployment and API integration, not a hosted consumer website.
